title: Epilobium pygmaeum
intro: Epilobium pygmaeum, also known as dwarf willowherb, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Onagraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote health and wellness, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Epilobium pygmaeum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Epilobium pygmaeum include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support urinary health and address conditions such as benign prostatic hyperplasia and urinary tract infections.
taxonomy: Epilobium pygmaeum is classified within the family Onagraceae and is part of the genus Epilobium. The genus Epilobium is a group of flowering plants commonly known as willowherbs or fireweeds. Epilobium pygmaeum is a small, annual plant that belongs to the order Myrtales. It is native to the alpine regions of the western United States and Canada. The species Epilobium pygmaeum is further divided into several subspecies based on its geographical distribution.
